content.full_text:
Re
Farn PolrrrcAI- PRACTTcES C oMMrssroN
P.O. Box 807 o 428 J Street r Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
May 30,2002
Carl Washington, Assemblymember
California Legislature
State Capitol
Post Office Box942849
Sacramento, CA 94249-000 1
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-02-140
Does the Act prohibit or limit you in any way from voting on or otherwise
participating in legislation, which may have a financial impact on the Compton Unified
School District, which has offered you employment after you vacate your office in
December of this year?
CONCLUSION
No. Section87407 of the Act prohibits a state officer or employee who has an
arrangement concerning prospective employment with an entity, from making a
governmental decision that will directly affect that entity. However, regulation
18747(d)(3) specifically states that the prohibition does not apply if the prospective
employer is a state, local, or federal government agency. Your prospective employer, the
Compton Unified School District, is considered a local government agency under section
82041of the Act and therefore, the prohibition of section87407 does not apply.
However, the one-year ban on lobbying the Legislature contained in section87406 of the
Act will apply to you when you leave the Legislature and start working for the Compton
Unified School District.
I Govemment Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
Dear Assemblyman Washington:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").I
QUESTION


File No. A-02-140
Page No. 2
FACTS
You request written advice on your prospective employment with the Compton
Unified School District. Specifically, you inquire whether the Act prohibits or fimlts you
in any way from voting or otherwise participating in legislation, which may have a
financial impact on a local school district that has offered you a position once you have
termed out of office in December 2002.
ANALYSIS
The analysis of this question is identical to the analysis in the advice letter sent
out to you on Aprrl 12,2002, and labeled as A-02-034a (enclosed). This analysis
included discussions on negotiating prospective employment (Section874O7), legislative
conflicts of interest (Sections 87102 5 - 87102.6), as well as the one-year lobbying ban
(Section 87406(b). Since that analysis was based on facts regarding a local
governmental agency (Compton Community College District) and this request for advice
is also regarding a local governmental agency as defined in section 82041of the Act
(Compton Unified School District), the analysis and conclusions are exactly the same.
Both analyses come to these conclusions: (1) Your prospective employer, the
Compton Unified School District, is considered a local government agency under section
82041of the Act and therefore, the prohibition of section87407 does not apply; (2) The
one-year ban on lobbying the Legislature contained in section 87406 of the Act will apply
to you when you leave the Legislature and start working for the Compton Unified Scliooi
District.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
By: Galena West
Staff Counsel, Legal Division
Enclosure

sections.question_synthetic: Does the Political Reform Act prohibit or limit a state legislator from voting on legislation that may financially impact a local government agency that has offered them prospective employment after leaving office?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The Political Reform Act does not prohibit voting on legislation affecting the prospective employer (Compton Unified School District) because it is a local government agency exempt from section 87407's restrictions. However, the one-year lobbying ban under section 87406 will apply once the legislator leaves office.

The QUESTION section header appears at end of document with no content below it. Facts and Analysis sections are present. References prior related advice letter A-02-034a regarding same requestor and similar Compton entity.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does the Political Reform Act prohibit or limit a state legislator from voting on legislation that may financially impact a local government agency that has offered them prospective employment after leaving office?

CONCLUSION: No. Section87407 of the Act prohibits a state officer or employee who has an
arrangement concerning prospective employment with an entity, from making a
governmental decision that will directly affect that entity. However, regulation
18747(d)(3) specifically states that the prohibition does not apply if the prospective
employer is a state, local, or federal government agency. Your prospective employer, the
Compton Unified School District, is considered a local government agency under section
82041of the Act and therefore, the prohibition of section87407 does not apply.
However, the one-year ban on lobbying the Legislature contained in section87406 of the
Act will apply to you when you leave the Legislature and start working for the Compton
Unified School District.
Dear Assemblyman Washington:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").I

embedding.summary: FPPC advises Assemblymember Carl Washington that he may vote on legislation affecting the Compton Unified School District despite pending employment there, as local government agencies are exempt from section 87407 restrictions. However, he will be subject to a one-year lobbying ban after leaving office.
